'''Vel Terno''' was a [[Colonel/Legends|colonel]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|navy]], and served as the [[Fighter wing/Legends|fighter wing]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] for the [[SilverWing Squadron|SilverWing]] and [[GoldWing Squadron|GoldWing]] [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]]<ref name="Orbit" /> in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].<ref name="Date">{{FarOrbitDate}}</ref> He demanded perfection from his [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], always drilling them, and maintained a patrol around the Imperial [[Bavos-I heavy-duty military space platform|Bavos-I military space platform]] [[Station 2LC/Blue]], located near the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rhinnal/Legends|Rhinnal]] in the [[Darpa sector/Legends|Darpa sector]]. Because of his leadership, any [[Starship/Legends|starship]] that approached the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] was met with capable [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. Terno was an ace fighter pilot, and had achieved fifteen combat [[Death/Legends|kills]]. Terno wore a [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]] and was equipped with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]].<ref name="Orbit" />

==Behind the scenes==
Vel Terno was mentioned in [[1998]]'s ''[[The Far Orbit Project]]'', by [[Timothy S. O'Brien]]. The book was a supplement to [[West End Games]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and was a sequel of sorts to<ref name="Orbit" /> the previous publication ''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'', carrying on that book's theme by following the privateering vessel ''Far Orbit''.<ref name="PP">''[[Pirates & Privateers]]''</ref>
